sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right knee pain
History of Present Illness
The patient reports persistent right knee pain for the past three months, worsening with activity and stairs. He initially injured the knee during a sporting event, but denies a specific injury. He describes the pain as a dull ache, with occasional sharp twinges. He has tried over-the-counter ibuprofen with limited relief. He denies any instability or giving way sensation.
Findings
The patient's right knee demonstrates mild tenderness to palpation over the medial joint line. Range of motion is limited by pain, with a decreased active range of motion. Lachman's test is negative. McMurray's test is negative. No effusion is palpable.
Assessment
The patient's presentation is most consistent with medial collateral ligament sprain.
Plan
Prescribe ice packs and elevation. Recommend physical therapy for range of motion and strengthening exercises. Advise the patient to avoid activities that exacerbate the pain. Follow up in two weeks to assess progress.
